symptoms.symptom_name: Chest pain during the tachycardic panic episode

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chest pain during tachycardia against a fixed obstruction is the cardinal warning of supply-demand ischaemia.
Chest tightness/pain is a common somatic feature of panic — but does not exclude the ischaemic mechanism here.
Pre-syncope with a fixed obstruction indicates compromised cardiac output — a haemodynamic red flag.
Pre-syncope is a common presentation of a primary arrhythmia and cannot be excluded without an ECG.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sustained tachycardia (~145 bpm, wearable) not settling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachycardia is the mechanism shortening diastolic coronary filling and raising oxygen demand against the fixed obstruction.
A sustained fast rate may be a primary arrhythmia rather than sinus tachycardia — ECG required to distinguish.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden loud siren immediately preceding onset (hyperacusis trigger)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A documented auditory trigger through known hyperacusis is the mechanistic driver of the sympathetic surge.
A clear external sensory trigger argues against a primary psychotic process.
Hyperventilation is a core panic feature and drives the somatic symptoms.
Fast deep breathing without wheeze argues against bronchospasm.

symptoms.symptom_name: Harsh systolic ejection murmur at the right upper sternal border radiating to the neck

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician auscultation confirms the fixed outflow obstruction — but this is a sealed clinician-only finding; the AI Doctor infers the obstruction from the reported cardiac history, not from a murmur it cannot hear.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals: HR 145, BP 140/90, RR 32, SpO2 98%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective sustained tachycardia and hypertension — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor works from reported palpitations and an unverified wearable reading, not a measured value.

investigation_details.expected_result: Sinus tachycardia; must be scrutinised for ischaemic ST/T changes or a primary tachyarrhythmia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: True ischaemia or arrhythmia is missed — the reason home management is unsafe.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An ECG is the minimum test needed to exclude ischaemia/arrhythmia and is unavailable in telehealth — hence escalation.
Clear chest on auscultation makes an asthma exacerbation unlikely as the cause of the breathlessness.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Demand-ischaemia danger cluster in fixed outflow obstruction
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: demand myocardial ischaemia secondary to tachycardia in supravalvular aortic stenosis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chest pain plus near-syncope plus sustained tachycardia against a fixed obstruction is the constellation that turns a 'panic attack' into a cardiac emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only way to exclude true ischaemia or a primary arrhythmia driving the tachycardia.
Detects arrhythmia and tracks the response as the heart rate settles.
Assesses for myocardial injury if ischaemia is suspected.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home/remote sedation attempt by the AI Doctor
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Paediatric sedation cannot be dosed or monitored remotely and would delay definitive assessment — escalate instead.
